titleStmt.title: Letter from Philip C. MacDermot to Henry Arthur Wynne, 28 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Philip C. MacDermot

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by Philip MacDermot (b. 1863), the crown solicitor for Roscommon. It is addressed to Sir Henry Arthur Wynne (1867 – 1945). Wynne was appointed to the vacant position of chief crown solicitor following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916).
Message requesting adjournment of a Ballinasloe cattle driving case as the counsel are engaged elsewhere.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
POST OFFICE TELEGRAPHS.
Message Received in Chief's Secretary's Office.
No. of Telegram
40
Service Instructions
Boyle OHMS
Office Stamp
Handed in at at 12pm Received here at } 12.31pm
TO {Chief Crown Solr Dublin
Castle Letter from District Inspr defendants
solr wants adjournment as counsel
engaged at galway assizes if
attorney general consents need I
go to Ballinasloe for tomorrow please
wire as soon as convenient
defendants solicitor wishes reply macdermott
